title: Improve active panel visual indicator in TUI
description: Improve visual feedback so the currently active panel is obvious at a glance, including stronger focus styling and clear status cues for active panel context.
acceptance_criteria: Active panel is visually distinct and unambiguous in normal terminal themes.
Focus state updates correctly when switching via keyboard and mouse.
README documents active-panel cues and focus behavior.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` so the active list/detail panel uses stronger visual chrome, panel titles show `[ACTIVE]` vs `[idle]`, list/detail renderers include explicit focus cues, and the status panel now reports the active interaction context. Added regression tests in `tests/test_tui.py` covering renderer cues plus keyboard and mouse focus updates. Documented the new active-panel cues and focus behavior in `README.md`.
handoff_summary.risks: Low conflict risk limited to concurrent edits in the same TUI/docs test files. Border-title rendering relies on Textual/Rich title handling, but targeted focus and rendering regressions passed.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Concurrent work touching `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, `tests/test_tui.py`, or `README.md` may need a small manual merge because this bead updates panel rendering, focus cues, and related docs/tests together.

conflict_risks: Concurrent work touching `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, `tests/test_tui.py`, or `README.md` may need a small manual merge because this bead updates panel rendering, focus cues, and related docs/tests together.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B0066

Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B0066-test

Improved TUI active-panel visibility with stronger focused styling, explicit active/idle panel labels, and a status-panel active-context line; added focused regression coverage and README documentation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
